title: quercus × allorgeana
intro: Quercus × allorgeana, also known as Allorge's oak, is a rare and hybrid deciduous tree that belongs to the Quercus genus, combining characteristics from the sessile oak (Quercus petraea) and the pedunculate oak (Quercus robur). The leaves and bark of Quercus × allorgeana have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and act as a natural remedy for digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional herbal medicine. Quercus × allorgeana can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and its leaves and bark can also be dried and used in teas, salves, and other herbal preparations.
